summary: A structured topic package with guided reading, answer-focus questions, and official free resource links.

materialSections.title: Read this first
materialSections.body: Start by reading how terms are expanded from brackets and then combined only when they are like terms.

materialSections.title: What to look for
materialSections.body: Watch for negative signs before brackets, coefficients outside brackets, and final expressions that are not fully simplified.

materialSections.title: Why this matters
materialSections.body: This topic appears across GCSE papers in direct simplification, equation solving, and multi-step modelling questions.

questions.answerFocus: Expand each bracket carefully, then combine the x terms and constants separately.

questions.prompt: Why is 3a + 2b not the same as 5ab?
questions.answerFocus: Explain the difference between addition of unlike terms and multiplication of variables.

questions.prompt: What mistake might a student make when expanding -2(x - 4)?
questions.answerFocus: Focus on the sign change across both terms inside the bracket.
